summary: Full backend + frontend regression for Gestor Financeiro MVP (auth allowlist, billings/expenses with recurrence + skips, summary, multi-tenant isolation). 15/15 backend pytest passed. All critical UI flows passed: login render, /dashboard auth redirect, dashboard testIds, create billing, create expense, toggle PAID/PENDING, month navigation.

minor.issue: Uses Depends(get_current_user) (returns 401 if session expired) and clears cookie only; does not delete the session row from user_sessions even though AuthRepository.delete_session exists. Stale tokens remain valid until natural expiry.

action_items: (Minor) /api/auth/logout: read session_token cookie/header and call auth_repo.delete_session(token) so the row is removed from MongoDB, not just the cookie cleared.

(Optional hardening) /api/auth/session does not validate that the Emergent OAuth response email matches the Google-authenticated user — currently relies solely on is_email_allowed. Already enforced via allowlist, no fix needed unless multiple emails are added later.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py: logout endpoint imports 'from fastapi import Request' inside the function but never uses Request; remove dead import.

domain/auth.py: AuthRepository.upsert_user mutates 'existing' dict in-place but then does 'User(**existing)' — fine, but the in-memory mutation does not match the DB doc projection (no created_at present in $set). created_at is preserved correctly because the doc is fetched first.
infrastructure/repositories.py: _month_filter correctly handles recurring start_year/start_month <= year/month and one-shot exact match; multi-tenant scoping by user_id verified across all queries.
Cookie config (SameSite=None; Secure; httpOnly; path=/) matches the cross-origin SPA pattern. OK.
Bearer token path in _extract_token + Cookie fallback works end-to-end. Verified.
Frontend Dashboard refresh() uses Promise.all and re-fetches after each mutation — UI stays in sync with summary card. Verified.
test_credentials: Auth uses Emergent Google OAuth (no password). For E2E we directly seed users + sessions in MongoDB per /app/auth_testing.md and /app/memory/test_credentials.md.
seed_data_creation: Test fixtures create two users (icaroomanuel@gmail.com + a 2nd test email) with bearer tokens directly in mongo at session scope; all TEST_-prefixed billings/expenses are deleted by the tests themselves; fixture teardown removes the seeded users/sessions/data. A separate frontend session was also seeded and cleaned up at the end.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Test file is at /app/backend/tests/test_api.py with conftest.py providing user_a/user_b/anon_client/client_a/client_b fixtures. Re-run with: REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL=$(grep REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL /app/frontend/.env | cut -d= -f2) MONGO_URL=mongodb://localhost:27017 DB_NAME=test_database pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v. For frontend, seed a session via the mongo snippet in /app/auth_testing.md, add cookie session_token to the preview domain, then navigate to /dashboard.
rca_of_issues: No blocking issues found. Only minor improvement is the logout endpoint not deleting the session row from MongoDB; functionally users are logged out client-side because the cookie is cleared, but the bearer token in Authorization header would still be valid until natural 7-day expiry. Low impact for current single-allowlisted-user setup.
